topic: A person's online friend turns out to live in the same city
raw_output:
[story 1]

I scrolled through the feed, a slow, deliberate motion, my jaw tightening almost imperceptibly. Another perfectly curated sunset photo from 'AstroDreamer,' complete with overly filtered golden hour lighting. I sighed, a sound that felt heavy in the quiet apartment. It was infuriating how effortlessly they seemed to inhabit a life of vibrant, spontaneous beauty, all while I was stuck staring at the grey concrete of my own reality. Then, a notification popped up: 'AstroDreamer' just posted a story from the city center. My breath hitched. The location tag wasn't vague; it was downtown. A cold knot formed in my stomach as I clicked. The skyline was unmistakable. It was *my* skyline. A strange, unwelcome realization washed over me, quickly followed by a surge of irritation.

[story 2]

"Seriously?" I muttered, tapping furiously on my phone screen, my fingers hovering over the 'unfollow' button. 'PixelPioneer' had been such a beacon of unattainable coolness online—always traveling, always in exotic locales. But the geotag attached to their latest video was bafflingly local. A familiar bridge, a recognizable coffee shop sign. My brow furrowed, a slight tremor running through my hand as I reread the coordinates. It couldn't be. The sheer absurdity of it made my mouth curl into a tight, unpleasant line. I leaned back in my chair, the cheap fabric squeaking under my weight, trying to rationalize the impossible coincidence.

[story 3]

The notification arrived like an unwelcome intrusion. It was a shared location pin, an accidental leak from a story posted by 'Wanderlust_Will.' My immediate reaction was to swipe it away, to dismiss it as a glitch in the algorithm. But the coordinates pointed directly to the neighborhood where I lived. A slow burn of annoyance started in my chest, escalating into a sharp, almost physical tension behind my eyes. I stared at the screen, my reflection in the dark glass mirroring a strained expression. It was impossible. The sheer audacity of this digital phantom suddenly manifesting in my physical vicinity felt deeply irritating, like a poorly executed prank I was forced to witness.
